---
title: "Curriculum Vitae [<i class='bi bi-download'></i>](Wassim_Kabalan_CV.pdf)"
description: "Wassim Kabalan — R&D Engineer & PhD Candidate (APC, CNRS/IN2P3)"
page-layout: article
toc: true
toc-title: "Contents"
number-sections: false
format:
  html:
    css:
      - ../assets/cv.css
---

# Wassim Kabalan

**R&D Engineer & PhD Candidate in Computational Cosmology**

I am an R&D Engineer and PhD Candidate at **APC (CNRS/IN2P3)**. My work bridges the gap between mathematical physics and industrial-grade software engineering. I specialize in **differentiable programming (adjoint-based methods)**, **simulation pipelines**, and **inverse problems** on massive GPU architectures.


[![GitHub - ASKabalan](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-ASKabalan-lightblue?style=flat&logo=github&logoColor=black)](https://github.com/ASKabalan/)
[![LinkedIn - Wassim Kabalan](https://img.shields.io/badge/LinkedIn-Wassim%20Kabalan-0A66C2?style=flat&logo=linkedin&logoColor=white)](https://www.linkedin.com/in/wassimkabalan/)
[![Email - wassim@apc.in2p3.fr](https://img.shields.io/badge/Email-wassim%40apc.in2p3.fr-blue?style=flat)](mailto:wassim@apc.in2p3.fr)

---


## Education

<table class="cv-experience">
<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Dec 2023 –<br>Oct 2026<br>(expected)</td>
<td class="cv-content">
**PhD, Physics of the Universe**, *Université Paris Cité*, Paris, France<br>
APC, CNRS/IN2P3<br>
Thesis: *Automatically differentiable and distributed probabilistic programming for weak-lensing inference.*<br>
Advisors: Eric Aubourg, Alexandre Boucaud, Josquin Errard, François Lanusse
</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Apr 2023 –<br>May 2023</td>
<td class="cv-content">**Advanced AI for Data Analysis**, *École Polytechnique (Executive Education)*</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Sep 2016 –<br>Sep 2018</td>
<td class="cv-content">**M2, Electronics, Electrical & Automation**, *Université Gustave Eiffel*</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Sep 2013 –<br>Nov 2016</td>
<td class="cv-content">**Licence, Engineering Science**, *Université Paris-Est Créteil (UPEC)*</td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Research & Professional Experience

<table class="cv-experience">
<tr>
  <td class="cv-date">Dec 2023 –<br>Present</td>
  <td class="cv-content">
    <strong>Graduate Researcher (PhD)</strong>, <em>APC, CNRS/IN2P3</em>, Paris, France<br>
    <em>Focus: Computational Cosmology, HPC & Inverse Problems</em>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Differentiable Simulation (LSST):</strong> Developing <strong>JaxPM</strong>, a differentiable N-body forward model enabling gradient-based calibration of high-dimensional physical models using adjoint methods (via JAX AD).</li>
      <li><strong>Inverse Problems (Simons Observatory):</strong> Solving large-scale inverse problems for CMB component separation. Implementing optimization workflows for <strong>spatially varying parameters</strong> to maximize signal recovery.</li>
      <li><strong>HPC Infrastructure:</strong> Author of <strong>jaxDecomp</strong>, enabling multi-node domain decomposition and distributed FFTs on GPU clusters (binding to NVIDIA cuDecomp).</li>
    </ul>
  </td>
</tr>

<tr>
  <td class="cv-date">Oct 2019 –<br>Oct 2023</td>
  <td class="cv-content">
    <strong>Software Infrastructure Engineer</strong>, <em>Dassault Systèmes</em>, Vélizy-Villacoublay, France<br>
    <ul>
      <li>Optimized CATIA cache/conversion pipeline (<strong>C++/Linux</strong>) for very large CAD data flows.</li>
      <li>Led Linux convergence for a large C++ rich client.</li>
      <li>Built <strong>GitLab CI/CD</strong> pipelines for automated testing, packaging, and releases.</li>
    </ul>
  </td>
</tr>

<tr>
  <td class="cv-date">Jan 2019 –<br>Oct 2019</td>
  <td class="cv-content">
    <strong>Data Acquisition Engineer</strong>, <em>SERMA (for Renault)</em>, Guyancourt, France<br>
    <ul>
      <li>Built tooling for high-volume vehicle sensor logs, including a custom <strong>C decoder</strong> for ECU binary data.</li>
      <li>Developed parallel Python post-processing workflows on an <strong>88-core server</strong> for vehicle network data.</li>
      <li>Automated conversion pipelines for vehicle network data.</li>
    </ul>
  </td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Supervision

<table class="cv-experience">
<tr>
<td class="cv-date">Jan 2025 –<br>Jun 2025</td>
<td class="cv-content">
**Binh Nguyen**, Master 1 Student<br>
*Project:* Inference of Weak-Lensing Parameters from Blended Galaxies Using Generative Neural Networks.<br>
*Outcome:* Talk at Rencontres du Vietnam.
</td>
</tr>
</table>

---

## Talks & Tutorials

- **Generative AI with JAX** [Tutorial] • AISSAI School 2025 • Oct 2025 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_10_AI)
- **JAXPM: Scalable and Differentiable Particle-Mesh Simulations** • Bayesian Deep Learning Workshop • May 2025 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_05_BDL/index.html#/title-slide)
- **Bayesian Inference for Cosmology with JAX** [Tutorial] • Bayesian Deep Learning Workshop • May 2025 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_05_BDL/index.html#/title-slide)
- **Massively Parallel Computing in Cosmology with JAX** [Tutorial] • CoPhy 2024 • Nov 2024 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_05_BDL/index.html#/title-slide)
- **Differentiable and Distributed Particle-Mesh N-body Simulations** • LSST France 2024 • Jun 2024 [slides](https://askabalan.github.io/slides/2025_05_BDL/index.html#/title-slide)

---

## Open-Source Software

- **[jax-fli](https://github.com/ASKabalan/jax-fli)** — **Author**. JAX-based Field Level Inference package with fully distributed and high-resolution N-body and lensing simulations.
- **[jaxDecomp](https://github.com/DifferentiableUniverseInitiative/jaxDecomp)** — **Author**. JAX bindings to NVIDIA cuDecomp for distributed 3D FFTs and multi-GPU domain decomposition.
- **[JaxPM](https://github.com/DifferentiableUniverseInitiative/JaxPM)** — **Main contributor/Maintainer**. Particle-mesh cosmological simulation toolkit in JAX with multi-accelerator support.
- **[FURAX](https://github.com/CMBSciPol/furax)** — **Main contributor/Maintainer**. Framework for Unified and Robust data Analysis with JAX for inverse problems in cosmology.
- **[FURAX_CS](https://github.com/CMBSciPol/furax-cs)** — **Main contributor/Maintainer**. Component separation pipeline for the Simons Observatory and LiteBird using FURAX.
- **[jax-healpy](https://github.com/CMBSciPol/jax-healpy)** — **Main contributor/Maintainer**. JAX-native HEALPix utilities — GPU-ready and differentiable.
- **[jax-grid-search](https://github.com/CMBSciPol/jax-grid-search)** — **Author**. Distributed grid search + gradient-based optimization built on JAX/Optax.

**Contributions**

- **[S2FFT](https://github.com/astro-informatics/s2fft)** — **Contributor**. Differentiable spherical & Wigner transforms (JAX & PyTorch). *Contribution:* CUDA spherical harmonics to reduce JAX JIT time.

---

## Publications

**Refereed**

- Spagnoletti, A., Boucaud, A., Huertas-Company, M., **Kabalan, W.**, and Biswas, B. 2024. [Bayesian Deconvolution of Astronomical Images with Diffusion Models: Quantifying Prior-Driven Features in Reconstructions](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.19158). arXiv:2411.19158 [astro-ph.IM].
  *Contribution:* Set up JAX-based deconvolution code and ran multi-node simulations on Jean Zay HPC cluster.

- Sommer, K., **Kabalan, W.**, and Brunet, R. 2024. [Infrared Radiometric Image Classification and Segmentation of Cloud Structure Using Deep-learning Framework for Ground-based Infrared Thermal Camera Observations](https://egusphere.copernicus.org/preprints/2024/egusphere-2024-101/). EGUsphere Preprint 2024-101.
  *Contribution:* Created and ran JAX-based U-Net model on Jean Zay HPC. Code: [github.com/ASKabalan/infrared-cloud-detection](https://github.com/ASKabalan/infrared-cloud-detection)

**Software**

- **Kabalan, W.**, Lanusse, F., Boucaud, A., and Aubourg, E. 2025. jaxDecomp: JAX Library for 3D Domain Decomposition and Parallel FFTs. Submitted to JOSS.

**Preprints**

- **Kabalan, W.**, Rizzieri, A., Sohn, W., Basyrov, A., Boucaud, A., Beringue, B., Chanial, P., Tsang King Sang, E., and Errard, J. 2026. [A GPU-Accelerated JAX Framework for Robust Parametric Component Separation and Clustering Optimization for CMB Polarization Satellites](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08463). arXiv:2604.08463 [astro-ph.CO].

**In Preparation**

- **Kabalan, W.**, Lanusse, F., Boucaud, A., and Aubourg, E. 2025. JAXPM: A JAX-Based Framework for Scalable and Differentiable Particle Mesh Simulations.




## Skills

**Programming**
Python (7y), JAX (3y), C++ (5y), CUDA (3y), PyTorch (1y)

**HPC & GPU Computing**
NCCL, MPI, Slurm, Nsight; multi-node GPU, distributed FFTs

**Statistics & Machine Learning**
Bayesian inference (MCMC, HMC, NUTS), simulation-based inference; NumPyro/BlackJAX

**DevOps & Software Engineering**
GitHub/GitLab CI, packaging (PyPI), containers, Linux, CMake, TDD

---

## Languages

<style>
  /* compact table rows */
  table.cv-experience.compact td {
    padding: 2px 8px;     /* was likely ~8–12px */
    line-height: 1.15;    /* tighter line height */
    vertical-align: top;
  }
  table.cv-experience.compact .cv-date {
    width: 120px;         /* tweak as you like */
    white-space: nowrap;
  }
</style>

<table class="cv-experience compact">
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">French</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Native</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">English</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Professional proficiency (C1)</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">Arabic</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Native</td>
  </tr>
  <tr>
    <td class="cv-date">German</td>
    <td class="cv-content">Basic (A2)</td>
  </tr>
</table>


---
